Agent: Third-generation SIN lentiviral vector (pLenti-CRISPR, HIV-1 backbone)
Justification: RG2 (documented attenuation from RG3) (replication-defective HIV-1 GMO). High-speed ultracentrifugation without sealed cups + 10⁸ TU/mL titer + 4 unfavorable factors → BSL-2+ with respiratory protection.

Agent: Mycobacterium tuberculosis complex (heat-inactivated specimens & purified DNA)
Justification: RG3 parental organism but ALL specimens heat-inactivated (56°C/30min) with culture-negative validation. Low PSR, 1 unfavorable factor. Enhanced BSL-2 proportionate; BSL-3 disproportionate.

Agent: E. coli BL21(DE3) recombinant protein expression
Justification: RG1 agent but sonication generates extreme aerosols at 10⁹–10¹⁰ CFU/mL. BSL-2 practices for sonication step despite RG1. Demonstrates PSR principle that even low-hazard agents warrant enhanced containment for high-aerosol procedures.
Single-round replication defect, cannot spread between cells. Small volumes (5–10 mL), BSC-contained. RG2 (documented attenuation from RG3) + Moderate PSR → BSL-2. Standard BSL-2 proportionate.

Agent: Recombinant AAV serotype 2 (dystrophin gene therapy vector)
Justification: Nominally RG2 but AAV extreme environmental stability (resistant to temp, pH, detergents) + 25 nm particle remains aerosolized + 10¹² particles/mL + 500 mL volume. 4 unfavorable factors → escalation. BSL-2+ with some protocols recommending BSL-3 for large-scale AAV.
Non-replicative protein–RNA complex. Transient intracellular activity, rapid degradation. No infectious capability. Closed electroporation. RG1 + Low PSR → BSL-1 with enhanced practices.
